channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: The Iran talks expose the collapse of US diplomacy
item.description: In the Trump era, American diplomacy is being led not by impartial diplomats, but by real estate investors.

item.title: Warmest June on record for England, second-warmest for UK, says Met Office
item.description: A punishing heatwave affected many parts of the country during the last week of the month.

item.title: Amnesty says RSF committed ethnic cleansing in Sudan’s el-Fasher
item.description: Rights group accuses the paramilitary of 'crimes against humanity' for attacks in and around North Darfur State capital.

item.title: What is a heat dome? The US heatwave explained
item.description: The heatwave will likely see record high temperatures, ahead of Fourth of July celebrations and FIFA matches in the US.

item.title: Secretive Gaza meetings in Cyprus as plan to isolate Hamas gathers pace
item.description: Board of Peace talks on Gaza's post-war future face funding crises and warnings of a colonial occupation.

item.title: Trump takes first flight on new Qatar-gifted Air Force One
item.description: US President Donald Trump took his maiden voyage aboard the new Air Force One on Wednesday.

item.title: Cape Verde: All to know before FIFA World Cup knockout against Argentina
item.description: An island nation of just over half a million inhabitants, Cape Verde pulled off the unthinkable by reaching the last 32.

item.title: Is Ukraine’s campaign of targeting Russian refineries working?
item.description: Fuel shortages in the country are unlikely to force Putin into concessions for peace.

item.title: ‘Will not leave’: Is Israel killing the US-Iran MoU by staying in Lebanon?
item.description: Analysts say both sides want the MOU to succeed but Israel's actions in Lebanon will prove a formidable obstacle.

item.title: First Syrian parliament since al-Assad’s ousting begins legislative duties
item.description: The new 210-member parliament takes shape, marking a historic shift after decades of Assad family rule.

item.title: India’s Bengal drops eggs from school lunches: Why that’s stoking debate
item.description: Opposition politicians say the BJP is 'imposing vegetarianism' by removing eggs from midday meals.

item.title: How war on Iran changed the global energy sector forever
item.description: Conflict will accelerate shift to renewables and search for alternatives to Strait of Hormuz, energy analysts say.

item.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian goalkeeper in Gaza
item.description: Palestinian goalkeeper Saleem Al-Ashqar was shot dead by Israeli forces in Gaza.

item.title: Sabalenka overcomes Kessler at Wimbledon, sets up Ostapenko clash
item.description: Top-seeded Aryna Sabalenka will play former French Open champion Jelena Ostapenko in a challenging last-32 matchup.

item.title: After Afghanistan fires drones into Pakistan, what’s next?
item.description: Pakistan and Afghanistan have been locked in an on-off cycle of cross-border strikes, leaving their ties in tatters.

item.title: Caught between the US and China, can Europe remain competitive?
item.description: As trade tensions grow, Europe tries to remain competitive while balancing ties with the US and China.

item.title: Colorado primary election results: Melat Kiros wins – key takeaways
item.description: Melat Kiros defeated Diana DeGette, who has been in office for as long as the 29-year-old Kiros has been alive.

item.title: Algeria heads to legislative polls amid record-low turnout fear
item.description: Post-Hirak distrust, candidate exclusions and political apathy are expected to drive low turnout.

item.title: Man charged after arson attack on Islamic centre in Dublin
item.description: A man set fire to an Islamic centre and mosque in Dublin, shouting Islamophobic and anti-immigrant slogans.

item.title: LIVE: England vs DR Congo – FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Live coverage and text updates from last 32 clash as England and DR Congo face off for place in round of 16.

item.title: UN Human Rights Council to hold urgent meeting on Sudan crisis
item.description: The emergency session follows warnings that 500,000 civilians around el-Obeid are at risk of large-scale atrocities.

item.title: US Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship: Who wins, who loses?
item.description: Court struck down US President Trump's executive order abolishing it, but he has pledged to challenge the ruling.

item.title: Who is Vadym Yermolaiev, the Ukrainian tycoon injured in Monaco blast?
item.description: Police in Monaco and France are hunting for a suspect seen leaving an explosive device at a residential building.

item.title: At least 6 killed in fire at apartment block in Belgium’s Antwerp
item.description: Firefighters battle blaze that has also injured a number of people in Linkeroever area of Antwerp.

item.title: Nearly three in four US scam victims report mental health harm, poll finds
item.description: Gallup survey says the financial toll of scams in the United States in 2025 was estimated at $68bn.
